div.p: Every PTRACE_SYSCALL resume produces TWO stops per syscall. Toggle tracks which stop you are at.

g.text: BUG SCENARIO — toggle missing or desynchronised
Green border = ENTRY stop (syscall about to execute)
Red border = EXIT stop (syscall just completed)
Red bg = BUG / wrong register / missing toggle
orig_rax = ALWAYS safe for syscall number (both stops)
rax | syscall number | same as orig_rax (not yet clobbered)
READ: orig_rax for syscall# WRITE: none
orig_rax | syscall number | PRESERVED by kernel (use this)
rdi | arg1 | MAY be clobbered by kernel
rsi | arg2 | MAY be clobbered by kernel
rdx | arg3 | MAY be clobbered by kernel
r10 | arg4 | MAY be clobbered by kernel
rcx | DESTROYED | kernel used for return address
r11 | DESTROYED | kernel saved RFLAGS here
READ: rax for retval USE: orig_rax for syscall# (still valid)
Same open() — but in_syscall never toggled
Bug variant 2: reading rax at entry (before kernel runs)
rax in [-4095, -1] | ERROR | negate → errno code
rax == -2 | ENOENT | No such file or directory
rax >= 0 | SUCCESS| value depends on syscall
rax == 0 | OK | e.g. close(), write() partial
rax == 3 | fd=3 | e.g. open() returned fd
rax < -4095 | VALID | large negative = valid data (mmap)

text.tspan: /* NO newline yet — wait for exit stop */
/* NO newline yet — wait for exit stop */

g.text: long syscall = state->current_syscall_nr; /* saved at entry */

text.tspan: /* stop 1: tracer thinks this is EXIT (in_syscall stuck at 0) */

g.text: reads rax → value is 2 (syscall# still there, kernel hasn't run yet)
→ prints: syscall(???) = 2 ← WRONG: 2 is open's number, not retval

text.tspan: /* stop 2: tracer thinks this is ENTRY (in_syscall stuck at 0→1 wrong) */

g.text: reads orig_rax → value is 2 (correct number accidentally)
→ prints: open( ← incomplete, waits for "exit" that already passed

text.tspan: /* OUTPUT: garbled, syscall numbers misidentified as return values */
/* open returning ENOENT shows as "syscall(2) = -2" NOT "-1 ENOENT" */
/* stop 1: tracer thinks this is EXIT (in_syscall stuck at 0) */
/* stop 2: tracer thinks this is ENTRY (in_syscall stuck at 0→1 wrong) */
/* OUTPUT: garbled, syscall numbers misidentified as return values */
/* open returning ENOENT shows as "syscall(2) = -2" NOT "-1 ENOENT" */
/* ENTRY stop — rax holds syscall# (same as orig_rax) */

g.text: if you read rax here: rax = 2 (open) — looks correct THIS time

text.tspan: /* But after execve() or signal, rax may hold PRIOR retval: */
/* e.g., prior syscall returned fd=7, rax=7 at next entry stop */
/* Reading rax at entry → thinks syscall# is 7 (pread64) — WRONG */
/* Rule: ALWAYS use orig_rax for syscall number, at BOTH stops */
/* ENTRY stop — rax holds syscall# (same as orig_rax) */
/* But after execve() or signal, rax may hold PRIOR retval: */
/* e.g., prior syscall returned fd=7, rax=7 at next entry stop */
/* Reading rax at entry → thinks syscall# is 7 (pread64) — WRONG */
/* Rule: ALWAYS use orig_rax for syscall number, at BOTH stops */
/* Called on every SIGTRAP|0x80 stop (PTRACE_O_TRACESYSGOOD set) */
/* now == 1 → this is an ENTRY stop */
/* now == 0 → this is an EXIT stop */
/* Called on every SIGTRAP|0x80 stop (PTRACE_O_TRACESYSGOOD set) */
/* now == 1 → this is an ENTRY stop */
/* now == 0 → this is an EXIT stop */
/* Kernel encodes errno as negative in [-4095, -1].
* Values outside this range are valid returns.
/* Kernel encodes errno as negative in [-4095, -1].
* Values outside this range are valid returns.
